macOS.__comment__signing: TODO(signing): populate `signingIdentity` with an Apple Developer ID Application certificate (e.g. `Developer ID Application: Voyagent, Inc. (TEAMID)`). Also set `providerShortName` and `entitlements` for notarization. CI reads APPLE_CERTIFICATE, APPLE_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD, APPLE_ID, APPLE_PASSWORD, APPLE_TEAM_ID from secrets — see apps/desktop/README.md.

windows.__comment__signing: TODO(signing): populate `certificateThumbprint` with the SHA-1 thumbprint of an EV or OV code-signing certificate installed in the build runner's cert store. CI reads WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE (base64 PFX) and WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE_PASSWORD from secrets — see apps/desktop/README.md.

updater.__comment__generate_keys: Run `pnpm tauri signer generate -w ~/.tauri/voyagent-updater.key` to generate an Ed25519 keypair. Copy the PUBLIC key into `pubkey` below; keep the private key OUT of the repo. CI exposes it as TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY + TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD env vars consumed by `tauri build`.
updater.__comment__manifest: The `endpoints` array is polled on launch. Host a JSON manifest describing the latest release (version, notes, per-platform download URL + signature). Tauri's docs show the schema: https://tauri.app/plugin/updater/. Replace the placeholder URL with your CDN / GitHub Releases endpoint before shipping.
updater.__comment__deploy: Manifest deploy flow: (1) `tauri build` emits *.sig files per artifact; (2) CI uploads artifacts + signatures to the CDN; (3) CI writes/updates latest.json with a new `version` and the base64 signatures from the *.sig files; (4) clients pick it up on next launch.
